rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into the target implementation scope when bindings require implementation-level context, preserving AADL component structure.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL scope boundaries: component type annexes must use only type-visible features; implementation annexes may bind subcomponent outputs and connections.
Only reference identifiers explicitly present in the AADL model—do not invent or infer ports, types, or packages.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax with valid scalar types: int, real, bool.
Guarantees and assumptions must reference only features visible at the annex scope—never subcomponent paths in type-level annexes.
anti_patterns: Do not embed AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables—these are not in scope unless exposed as typed ports.
Do not encode event ports as numeric or Boolean values—omit such clauses unless explicit data values are provided in scope.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
